title: Google L5 SWE Onsite Interview Experience
description: Faced 2 coding rounds (heavily on DP and Graphs), 1 System Design (Design a scalable notifications dispatcher), and 1 Googleyness behavioral round. Key takeaway: explain trade-offs of every approach you mention rather than just rushing to coding.
author: Rahul Sharma

commentsList.author: Arjun Patel
commentsList.text: Congrats on the offer! What system design resources did you use?

commentsList.author: Rahul Sharma
commentsList.text: Mainly DDIA book, ByteByteGo, and standard system design primer resources on GitHub.

title: Handling production emergency scenarios in behavioral interviews
description: Always structure situational answers using the STAR method. For production emergency questions, clarify how you stabilized the system first (monitoring, rolling back commits, routing around failures) before doing root cause post-mortems.
author: Sarah Jenkins

title: Stripe Machine Learning Engineer Onsite Questions
description: Focused extensively on practical ML system architectures: designing a transaction fraud detection system. Make sure you discuss latency limits, real-time vs batch feature stores, and how you evaluate model drift over time.
author: Devore Vance
